continuity_cautions: Older planning artifacts still contain stale Σ numbering and place NATURAL_LANGUAGE_TASK_BRIDGE later in the series.
The active prompt, completed Σ-1 mirror layer, and completed Φ-0 through Φ-2 runtime doctrine establish this bridge now as the current local checkpoint law.
Stale planning sequence text must not override active checkpoint law during task-intent normalization.
authority_order: scope-specific canonical planning, semantic, runtime, schema, contract, release, and policy artifacts
derived mirrors and generated operational artifacts

normalization_rules.summary: Treat natural language as input to classify, not project law.
Words such as runtime, package, service, release, fix, and bridge are hints only.
The same request may normalize differently depending on the current checkpoint and completed doctrine.
Affected roots, artifact families, and governing doctrine outrank urgency or broad wording.
Classify one primary family plus secondary families and review-gate flags when a request contains multiple intents.
Prefer narrower, review-aware, or planning-first classification when uncertainty remains.
Natural-language phrasing may not override ownership-sensitive root distinctions.

terms.required_meaning: constitutional host for lawful deterministic runtime execution
bounded coordinated runtime-facing mediation or hosting structure
terms.forbidden_collapse: component, generic orchestrator, or control-plane service

terms.required_meaning: semantic unit governed by domain contracts
client, server, launcher, setup, tools, appshell, or shell surface
declarative or authored content and capability expansion surface

ownership_cautions.summary: fields/ is canonical semantic field substrate; field/ remains transitional.
schema/ is canonical semantic contract law; schemas/ remains projection or advisory mirror.
packs/ and data/packs/ remain scoped ownership surfaces and are not interchangeable.
Canonical artifacts outrank projections, mirrors, caches, and generated evidence.
Natural-language runtime wording does not crown runtime/ as canonical.
Older planning numbering or order drift does not override active checkpoint law.

task_families.description: Sequence, checkpoint, dependency, continuity, and gate work.
runtime_vocabulary_alignment_notes: Do not promise runtime kernel, component, or service outcomes unless those surfaces already exist canonically.
review_escalation_rules: Escalate if sequence, authority ordering, or gates would be changed rather than described.

task_families.description: Normative semantic, runtime, contract, governance, or policy specification work.
runtime_vocabulary_alignment_notes: Do not let runtime or product wording redefine doctrine scope.
review_escalation_rules: Escalate if canon, glossary, or foundational ownership meaning would change.

task_families.description: Governance, mirror, instruction, refusal, review, and operator or agent policy work.
runtime_vocabulary_alignment_notes: Mirror and task-governance wording must reflect post-Φ-A1 kernel/component/service distinctions.
review_escalation_rules: Escalate if canonical governance or authority order would be reinterpreted.

task_families.description: Reality, domain, capability, bridge, formalization, representation, and semantic-law work.
runtime_vocabulary_alignment_notes: Runtime-layer terms may appear, but semantic doctrine remains upstream of runtime classification.
review_escalation_rules: Escalate if bridge law, ownership law, or foundational semantic meaning would move.

task_families.description: Kernel, component, service, persistence, replay, sandbox, and runtime-boundary work.
runtime_vocabulary_alignment_notes: Kernel, component, service, module, domain, product, and pack/content must remain distinct.
Natural-language runtime wording does not imply runtime/ is canonical.
review_escalation_rules: Escalate if runtime work would redefine semantic doctrine, governance law, or ownership review outcomes.

task_families.description: Client, server, launcher, setup, tools, and app-shell work.
authority_requirements: runtime doctrine when runtime boundaries are implicated
runtime_vocabulary_alignment_notes: Products consume kernel/components/services through lawful boundaries and do not own those meanings by default.
review_escalation_rules: Escalate if product wording tries to redefine runtime law or semantic law.

task_families.description: Release, publication, identity, trust, archive, update, and distribution work.
runtime_vocabulary_alignment_notes: A runtime service is not automatically a release or control-plane service.
review_escalation_rules: Escalate for publication, licensing, trust, public policy, or live distribution meaning.

task_families.description: Verification, proof, consistency, diagnostics, and audit work.
authority_requirements: task-relevant tests, proofs, and canonical doctrine
runtime_vocabulary_alignment_notes: Validation may inspect kernel/component/service boundaries without redefining them.
review_escalation_rules: Escalate when findings imply doctrine drift, ownership conflict, or hidden risk.

task_families.description: Physical convergence, merge-later execution, replacement, and structural normalization work.
runtime_vocabulary_alignment_notes: Do not let runtime or product wording hide structural replacement risk.
review_escalation_rules: Always review-gated when it crosses split roots, replace-classified subsystems, or multiple authority layers.

task_families.description: Checkpoint bundles, reports, manifests, upload packages, and temporary support artifacts.
runtime_vocabulary_alignment_notes: Packaging support artifacts do not define runtime architecture or release identity.
review_escalation_rules: Escalate only if bundling would touch canonical doctrine, release policy, or protected roots beyond support-artifact scope.

task_families.description: Explanation, walkthrough, summarization, and docs that do not change doctrine meaning.
runtime_vocabulary_alignment_notes: Explanations must preserve kernel/component/service distinctions rather than flattening them.
review_escalation_rules: Escalate if the requested documentation would actually change governance, semantic, runtime, or release meaning.

task_families.description: Investigation, codebase reading, classification, and non-mutating inspection work.
runtime_vocabulary_alignment_notes: Inspection may analyze kernel/component/service distinctions but does not edit them by default.
review_escalation_rules: Escalate if analysis findings imply protected-root changes, doctrine reinterpretation, or replacement work.

global_review_escalation_rules: changes touching canon doctrine or glossary meaning
release, publication, licensing, trust, or public policy meaning
late Ζ or live-ops style work
broad refactors crossing multiple authority layers
foundational semantic or runtime boundary reinterpretation outside the active doctrine prompt

representative_examples.request: Create a checkpoint review for the current post-Φ state.
Formalize a new bridge doctrine between ecology and logistics.
Define the next runtime persistence service boundary.
Update the Copilot mirror to reflect the new service model.
Make me a tmp zip for this checkpoint.
Freeze release-index rules for update channels.
Merge schema and schemas and clean up the repo.
